data.title: As a developer, all gate and validate scripts that hardcode ruff or format-check read commands.lint/format_check instead
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Audit and update all scripts beyond auto-format.sh, validate.sh, gate-2b, and gate-2d that invoke ruff check, mypy, or format-check unconditionally. Known candidates: validate-phase.sh, merge-to-main.sh, smoke-test-portable.sh. Either route through commands.lint/format_check or add [DSO WARN]+skip guard. Scripts that are intentionally Python-only must be documented with a rationale comment.

**Why**: The named scripts (S1-S3) make the primary gates polyglot, but residual hardcoded ruff/format-check calls in other scripts would leave non-Python projects partially unaddressed and undermine the epic's stack-agnostic goal.

**Scope**:
- IN: grep sweep of plugins/dso/scripts/ for unconditional ruff check/ruff --fix/prettier --check; updates to any discovered scripts; documentation of intentionally Python-only paths
- OUT: auto-format.sh, validate.sh, gate-2b, gate-2d (covered by S1-S3); onboarding (covered by S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, grep for unconditional ruff check, ruff --fix, and prettier --check calls across plugins/dso/scripts/ returns no results outside validate.sh, auto-format.sh, gate-2b, gate-2d, and ruff-specific legacy fallback paths
  ← Satisfies: "Epic goal: stack-agnostic gate pipeline"
- When this story is complete, any discovered scripts either read commands.lint or commands.format_check with a WARN+skip guard, or contain an inline comment explaining why they are intentionally Python-only
  ← Satisfies: "Epic goal: stack-agnostic gate pipeline"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for any modified scripts

## Considerations

- [Residual References] validate-phase.sh, merge-to-main.sh, and smoke-test-portable.sh are the candidates identified by red team analysis — confirm with grep at implementation time before modifying any files

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
